summary: All code_refs and Deepgram-ebook figures/quotes verified against ground truth. Every cited line shows the claimed mechanism or value. No WRONG or MISCITED claims.

claims.found: 170: async def ulaw_to_pcm(ulaw_bytes, in_rate, out_rate, resampler)

claims.claim: audioop.ulaw2lin(ulaw_bytes, 2) at line 185 and audioop.lin2ulaw(in_pcm_bytes, 2) at line 209
Telnyx inbound_encoding/outbound_encoding default 'PCMU' at telnyx.py:62-63; telnyx_sample_rate=8000 at :60
claims.found: 60: telnyx_sample_rate: int = 8000; 62: inbound_encoding: str = "PCMU"; 63: outbound_encoding: str = "PCMU". PCMA noted in docstring.

claims.claim: Cloudflare ships hand-written 256-entry MULAW_DECODE_TABLE / encodeMulaw (index.ts:40,64) because Workers lacks audioop
claims.found: 42: const MULAW_DECODE_TABLE = new Int16Array(256); 64: export function encodeMulaw(sample: number). Table built in loop over 256 entries. (Decl at 42 not 40, encodeMulaw at 64 exact — within a few lines.)

claims.claim: Exotel diverges: carries raw 16-bit linear PCM, exotel_sample_rate=8000 at :49, resamples PCM<->PCM with no companding
claims.found: 49: exotel_sample_rate: int = 8000; only create_stream_resampler used (lines 72-73,101,141); no audioop/ulaw/alaw calls. 100: comment 'Exotel outputs PCM audio'; 150: 'Exotel takes PCM data'.

twilio.py:54 twilio_sample_rate=8000 + pcm_to_ulaw; plivo.py:54 plivo_sample_rate=8000 + audio/x-mulaw; genesys.py:148 genesys_sample_rate=8000, :151 media_format default PCMU; telnyx.py:60 8000 + PCMU.

claims.claim: PSTN/SIP audio operates at 8 kHz, mono, using mu-law or A-law PCM
claims.found: 716: 'Telephony audio operates at 8 kHz, mono, using μ-law or A-law PCM.'

claims.claim: Format alignment is most important optimization; avoid transcoding; Deepgram models accept mu-law natively; TTS emits mu-law to gateways
claims.found: 719-721: 'The most important optimization is format alignment. Avoid transcoding wherever possible. Deepgram's speech models accept μ-law PCM natively, and its TTS can emit μ-law audio directly consumable by telephony gateways.'

claims.claim: Deepgram warns 8 kHz 'slightly increases recognition difficulty' (line 717)
claims.found: 717-718: '...slightly increases recognition difficulty compared to broadband sources.' (text wraps 717->718)

claims.claim: Bandwidth ceiling is a hard fidelity cap (note's framing of source)
claims.found: 717: 'This limited bandwidth places a hard ceiling on acoustic fidelity'

claims.claim: mu-law is logarithmic 8-bit companding of 16-bit PCM, 2:1 size reduction, irreversible quantization (mechanism)
claims.found: Consistent with ulaw2lin/lin2ulaw (8-bit<->16-bit, width=2) in utils.py and the Cloudflare MULAW_DECODE_TABLE (256-entry 8-bit -> Int16 mapping, lossy). G.711 mu-law is indeed lossy 8-bit log companding of 16-bit linear.

claims.claim: Pipecat transcodes to int16 internally because pipeline is PCM-only
claims.found: All telephony serializers (twilio/plivo/telnyx/genesys) call ulaw_to_pcm/pcm_to_ulaw at the wire boundary and feed int16 PCM into the pipeline; ulaw2lin(...,2) produces 16-bit linear. Architecture is PCM-internal.
